summary: Internal-API 401 token-refresh retry gated on idempotent methods, mirroring the public-client 5xx gate.
reason: doWithEncodedBody used to retry the original request after a 401-triggered token refresh regardless of method. 401s typically fire before body processing, so a POST retry is normally safe, but a token expiring mid-handler could leave a successful POST followed by a 401, and the retry would double-commit. Limit auto-retry to GET/PUT/DELETE/HEAD/OPTIONS; non-idempotent callers surface the 401 and re-run if appropriate. Pre-emptive fix for the pattern Greptile would likely flag on its next pass.
